label: Data Poisoning III
label_note: Clean-label backdoors are the stealthiest corner of poisoning, and detection work here still trails the attack literature by a wide margin.

label: Vision-Language Models
label_note: Mostly capability work; its security relevance is the rapidly expanding attack surface these multimodal stacks expose.

label: Watermarking I
label_note: LLM text watermarking has entered its adversarial phase - stealing and scrubbing attacks are currently winning against the detectability-robustness-quality trade-off.

label: Agent Security I
label_note: The densest, fastest-moving subfield in the graph; benchmarks (AgentDojo, AgentHarm, ASB) matured faster than any deployable defense.

label: Diffusion Image Forensics
label_note: Detecting diffusion-generated images works in-distribution, but generalization to unseen generators remains the open problem everyone claims to solve.

label: LLM Vulnerability Detection
label_note: LLMs find shallow bugs impressively and deep bugs rarely; evaluation leakage still inflates most reported numbers.

label: Membership Inference
label_note: Post-'First Principles' the field has rigorous foundations, but LLM-scale membership inference remains borderline ineffective in realistic settings.

label: AI for 6G Networks
label_note: Survey-heavy and forward-looking; concrete AI-security results for 6G/ISAC are thin relative to the volume of roadmap papers.

label: LLM Data Privacy
label_note: Attack papers show real leakage from conversations and fine-tunes, while deployed mitigations remain mostly policy and prompt hygiene.

label: Federated Learning Privacy
label_note: Enormous survey output; the interesting work is where privacy accounting meets realistic cross-silo deployments, which is a small minority.

label: Machine Unlearning
label_note: Unlearning is easy to claim and hard to verify - adversarial evaluations (WMDP, relearning attacks) keep falsifying certified-forgetting claims.

label: GAN Image Inpainting
label_note: Singleton cluster; an artifact of clustering rather than a real subfield.

label: LLM Security Surveys
label_note: A survey-and-benchmark catch-all; useful as maps, but the primary results live in the specialized attack clusters.

label: GNN Security
label_note: GNN attack papers greatly outnumber deployments; the robustness work is solid but the threat models are often unrealistic.

label: Safety Alignment I
label_note: Benchmark saturation is the story - safety evals multiply faster than alignment depth improves, and shallow alignment ('few tokens deep') is the field's own diagnosis.

label: Synthetic Image Detection
label_note: CLIP-based detectors set a strong baseline, but every 'sanity check' paper finds the previous state of the art overfit to known generators.

label: LLM Information Extraction
label_note: Application-driven; the security-relevant slice is threat-intel knowledge-graph construction, which is promising but under-evaluated.

label: Autonomous Vehicle Security
label_note: Broad AV safety/security mix; scenario-generation work is maturing while classical sensor-attack research has plateaued.

label: ML Side-Channel Attacks
label_note: Deep learning has quietly become the default side-channel analysis tool; the ML-for-hardware-Trojan-detection wing is far noisier.

label: Medical LLMs
label_note: Clinical capability work with a governance tail; the security-relevant privacy and safety analysis is still mostly aspirational.

label: Safety Alignment II
label_note: Agent-safety benchmarks (R-Judge, Agent-SafetyBench) show even frontier models fail basic risk awareness; the 'safetywashing' critique applies broadly here.

label: AI Privacy Governance
label_note: High-volume, low-signal governance surveys; a handful of grounded studies hide in a sea of boilerplate.

label: Smart Contract Security
label_note: LLM-based detectors (GPTScan lineage) are genuinely useful for logic bugs - a rare case of LLMs beating static analysis where it matters.

label: Data Poisoning I
label_note: Backdoor attacks on NLP and code models are thoroughly explored; trigger stealthiness has outpaced every published detector.

label: Time-Series Anomaly Detection
label_note: Reconstruction-based methods dominate; flawed benchmarks and metrics are the field's openly acknowledged dirty secret.

label: SDN DDoS Defense
label_note: Incremental ML-for-DDoS work; heavy on architecture diagrams, light on adversarial evaluation.

label: Deepfake Detection
label_note: Generalization to unseen forgery methods remains unsolved; most reported gains come from data augmentation, not architectural insight.

label: Smart Grid Resilience
label_note: Energy-systems ML with a security veneer; the rigorous false-data-injection work lives in other clusters.

label: Safety Alignment III
label_note: The RLHF mechanics wing: reward models are the weakest link, and reward hacking is now documented at frontier scale.

label: LLM Unlearning
label_note: The LLM-specific unlearning wave; MUSE-style multi-metric evaluation exposed how cosmetic most published forgetting is.

label: AI Cyber Defense
label_note: Survey-dominated XAI-for-IDS literature; explainability claims rarely survive contact with real SOC workflows.

label: Jailbreak Attacks I
label_note: The multi-turn and self-defense wing; Crescendo showed conversational escalation beats single-shot filters.

label: Blockchain Federated Learning
label_note: Blockchain-plus-FL hybrids are numerous and mostly architectural; concrete, measured security wins are scarce.

label: Agent Security II
label_note: Agentic-AI risk surveys and governance; long on taxonomy, short on measured attacks - the empirical work is in Agent Security I.

label: Differential Privacy
label_note: The one privacy subfield with real theory; the frontier is DP at LLM scale and side channels that bypass the formal model.

label: Vision-Language Attacks
label_note: Visual adversarial jailbreaks proved multimodality is a safety regression; VLM attacks are now where text jailbreaks were in 2023.

label: Hallucination Detection
label_note: Internal-state detectors (semantic entropy, INSIDE) are the most promising line; the 'hallucination is inevitable' results set useful theoretical bounds.

label: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
label_note: RAG methods work; the privacy and integrity risks of retrieval stores are only beginning to be quantified.

label: DL Vulnerability Detection
label_note: The pre-LLM deep-learning detectors; the 'how far are we' evaluations mostly answer 'not far' - datasets remain the bottleneck.

label: Watermarking II
label_note: The theory wing (undetectable and provable watermarks); elegant results whose robustness under paraphrase attack is still contested.

label: Model Inversion Attacks
label_note: Two-paper micro-cluster; the substantive inversion work is scattered across the privacy clusters.

label: Data Poisoning II
label_note: Poisoning of deployed systems (recommenders, medical KBs, FL); 'unlearning fails to remove poisoning' is the result to know.

label: Phishing Detection
label_note: Feature-engineering-era phishing ML persists; LLM-based detection is promising but evaluated on stale corpora.

label: Adversarial Examples
label_note: The classical adversarial-ML core; certified patch defenses are the rare defensive success in an attack-dominated decade.

label: RAG Security
label_note: RAG poisoning and jamming attacks (BadRAG, Phantom) landed fast and hard; retrieval-store integrity is now the weakest link in deployed LLM apps.

label: Jailbreak Attacks IV
label_note: The optimization wing (GCG lineage, COLD, AmpleGCG); transferable universal suffixes remain effectively undefended in open models.

label: Agent Security III
label_note: MCP security is 2025's gold rush - audits found real exploits, and protocol-level fixes are arriving slower than adoption.

label: Jailbreak Attacks II
label_note: In-the-wild and persuasion jailbreaks; human-derived social engineering still beats gradient methods against deployed systems.

label: Reward Modeling
label_note: Self-rewarding and reward-model work; reward hacking and obfuscation under monitoring are the security-relevant findings.

label: FL Intrusion Detection
label_note: Federated IDS papers multiply combinatorially (FL x IDS x IoT); few control honestly for non-IID data.

label: GNN Privacy
label_note: A small, technically solid niche - differential privacy on graphs is genuinely harder than on tabular data, and this cluster knows it.

label: Adversarial ML
label_note: The survey-and-taxonomy layer over the adversarial core; read the Adversarial Examples cluster for the primary results.

label: Watermarking III
label_note: Model and media watermarking for IP protection; robustness against extraction attacks (MEA-Defender) is the interesting frontier.

label: Malware Detection
label_note: Deep-learning malware detection is mature and plateaued; concept drift and adversarial evasion remain the honest open problems.

label: Prompt Injection
label_note: The defining LLM-app vulnerability: benchmarks are strong, prompt-level defenses fail systematically, and design-level isolation (CaMeL lineage) is the credible path.

label: IoT Intrusion Detection
label_note: High-volume applied IDS work; an accuracy race on stale datasets with little adversarial rigor.

label: AI Food Systems
label_note: Agrifood AI surveys; present in the graph via sustainability-security framing, marginal to the core taxonomy.

label: Jailbreak Attacks III
label_note: The black-box query wing (PAIR, JailbreakBench); twenty-query jailbreaks made query efficiency the metric that matters.

label: Blockchain-AI Integration
label_note: Blockchain-AI convergence surveys; volume without a driving empirical result.
